title: CI fail-fast:false is a temporary diagnostic setting left in ci.yml
evidence: fail-fast: false at ci.yml:16 with an in-file comment saying to restore to default (true) once the matrix is green; matrix is now green at bebb520
impact: Diagnostic-only; on green it wastes CI minutes running all cells after a failure and departs from intended steady-state signal. Not a correctness risk.
recommended_action: Restore fail-fast to default (remove the override) now that all 15 cells are green; commit; re-confirm green.

title: Secret-scan candidates are synthetic test fixtures (already baselined)
evidence: gitleaks (authoritative, 372 commits): no leaks found. Built-in scanner flagged 22 high = AKIA1234567890123456 / ghp_12345678901234567890 inside test_scan_and_redact_secrets and history; these are fabricated fixtures for ocman's own scan_for_secrets tests, baselined in .gitleaksignore by fingerprint.
impact: No real secret exposure. Confirmed false positives.
recommended_action: No action; baseline already in place from prior run.

title: macOS firmlink import-rebase fix (delta review) is correct and well-scoped
evidence: Delta since prior GO is exactly this fix + vistab floor. _rebased_dir resolves stored dir before matching resolved orig_worktree; lexical fallback preserves prior behavior for unresolvable/unrelated dirs (never rebases something that should not be). Resource handling (zipfile ctx mgr, conn.close in finally, txn + rollback backup) unchanged.
impact: The macOS bug (sessions left under old worktree on project import) is fixed; no data-loss/overwrite was ever involved. No MEM/LIVE regression.
recommended_action: Already fixed in 4cfcd18; verified in this review.
validation: full suite 408 passed; OS-agnostic regression test present + mutation-checked

title: Delta test changes are portability substitutions; assertions not weakened; new regression test present
evidence: test_move/test_export_import/test_file_tools/test_migrate: hardcoded POSIX paths -> abs_path() with expected values updated in lockstep (exact-equality checks preserved, incl. unrelated-dir-untouched negatives). Symlink tests use make_symlink (real link where OS allows, faithful sim on unprivileged Windows) instead of raw os.symlink, keeping guards under test cross-platform. New OS-agnostic regression test test_project_import_rebases_when_worktree_canonicalizes guards S2-B1 (mutation-checked).
impact: Regression protection for the delta is present and honest; no coverage silently dropped.
validation: 408 passed, 2 skipped (Linux py3.14); CI 15/15 green at bebb520

title: CHANGELOG [1.2.0] docs honestly reflect the delta (firmlink fix + vistab floor)
evidence: CHANGELOG [1.2.0] Fixed section documents both the macOS firmlink import-rebase fix and the vistab>=1.3.0 floor; [Unreleased] is empty; DECISIONS.md has dated entries for every delta decision incl. the fail-fast restore follow-up. Em/en dash scan clean (only sanctioned exceptions: verbatim attribution line, and AGENTS.md's own description of the rule).
impact: Docs are honest and cold-start-complete for the delta.

title: CHANGELOG [1.2.0] release date (2026-07-19) predates the finalized release
evidence: Header reads '## [1.2.0] - 2026-07-19' but the delta commits (firmlink fix, vistab floor, CI) landed 2026-07-20 and the tag has not been cut. The dated line should match the actual release/tag date.

recommended_action: Update the [1.2.0] date to the actual tag date at release time (Section 9), or now to 2026-07-20.
next_step: fix at tag time (S9) or in S7

title: vistab>=1.3.0 floor is correct and the package builds/imports cleanly
evidence: Installed vistab 1.3.0 imports cleanly (Set NameError fixed; 'Set' now in vistab API) and provides Vistab.set_color/set_header_style INSTANCE methods that ocman uses at cli.py:4758-4760 (the CHANGELOG's 'module-level' phrasing is imprecise but harmless). Clean build produced ocman-1.2.0 sdist+wheel; wheel contains ocman, ocman_tui, and the migrate script.
impact: Packaging is release-ready; the dependency floor resolves the Python 3.12 clean-install break.
validation: python -m build OK; import OK; PyPI published=1.1.0 < 1.2.0 (valid bump)
